note: the jump from 24 to 96 with almost nothing in between — scale contrast is doing all the talking here
title: type scale study

note: four mud tones that have no business working together and absolutely do. saving for the next time someone asks for 'pops of color'
title: warm neutrals

note: three strict columns and the photo breaks the grid exactly once. restraint, then a single violation — that's what makes it feel designed
title: editorial spread

note: monospace everything plus a dotted tear line. utilitarian objects are allowed to be beautiful and this one knows it
title: ticket stub

note: this gradient feels like 6am. want exactly this temperature for an empty state someday
the type IS the layout — rotate one word and the whole poster is done. confidence as a design system
title: swiss poster

note: the middle tier is just slightly taller and outlined darker. that's the entire upsell strategy — no badge, no ribbon, no 'most popular'
title: pricing tiers

note: drew the ampersand bigger than the canvas. cropped letterforms stop being letters and start being texture
title: ampersand

note: primary shapes, secondary palette. feels like a children's book for adults and I mean that as the highest compliment
zero shadows on the whole page — separation is hairlines only, and the active card just gets a darker line. stealing this
title: card stack

note: one bar, one dot, eighty percent emptiness. the margin is the design
title: ma

note: dark UI where the only saturated color is the data line itself. the chrome fully recedes — this is what 'content first' actually looks like
title: dashboard

note: pull quote set in italic at 4x body size with a tiny red rule. wish I'd stopped over-decorating quotes years ago
a color ramp displayed as a grid of dots instead of a boring bar. data as ornament
title: ramp study

note: type cranked up until it clips the edges, repeated until it's a pattern. ugly on purpose and somehow completely confident
title: brutalist poster

note: the dimension marks are prettier than the thing being dimensioned. annotations as the aesthetic
title: blueprint
